benefits.title: King’s can expand experiential learning consistently, inclusively and with impact
benefits.statement:
King’s has a shared educational and design basis from which to grow experiential learning across curriculum and co-curriculum. Educators, programme teams and professional services can draw on common principles, delivery models, assessment approaches and quality expectations rather than redesigning provision from first principles or relying on unsupported local practice.

This creates the preconditions for more expansive student access to experiential learning while improving consistency, inclusion and assurance of impact. It enables institutional support, investment and operating infrastructure to be organised around common ways of working, reducing duplication and making successful approaches easier to adapt and extend across different disciplines and contexts.
benefits.beneficiary: Educators, programme teams, faculties, professional services and, through subsequent opportunity growth, students
benefits.benefitType: institutional capability, inclusive growth and effective use of resource
benefits.successLooksLike: Teams developing experiential learning can use and adapt shared institutional models with confidence. Downstream investment, operating support and opportunity growth align with the framework; unnecessary reinvention is reduced; and new provision is designed from the outset around educational purpose, accessibility, quality and evidence of impact.

benefits.title: King’s establishes a distinctive position of thought and practice leadership
benefits.statement:
King’s develops a recognised intellectual and practical contribution to experiential learning at a point when the sector is rapidly converging on an insufficiently interrogated consensus centred on employability, placements and visible volumes of activity.

By coalescing its existing expertise, advancing its theoretical basis, testing its ideas through institutional practice and sharing what it learns, King’s can offer a richer and more credible account of experiential education. This strengthens the University’s reputation, supports external influence and creates opportunities for scholarship, collaboration and partnership.
benefits.beneficiary: King’s, its academic and professional community, students, external partners and the wider higher education sector
benefits.benefitType: thought leadership, external influence and institutional reputation
benefits.successLooksLike: King’s is recognised as a serious thought-and-practice leader in experiential learning. Its scholarship, framework and institutional experience are visible in sector debate, generate external engagement and collaboration, and influence how experiential learning is understood and developed beyond the University.
